cityDNA.coreFantasy: A dragonkin stormfire harbor city where shelter halls, roadside shrines, command bastions, containment keeps, harbor basilicas, and an egg-gate wonder turn danger into civic protection.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Vyrath’Kai is not just dragon spectacle; the uploaded images show a coastal fortress-city of shelter, shrine, command, containment, and harbor ritual.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Vyrath’Kai turns dragon-scale authority, stormfire memory, harbor routes, shelters, shrines, command law, containment ethics, and public crossings into a disciplined dragonkin civic order.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Basalt coastal terraces, stormfire glow, bronze and dark stone, dragon-scale patterns, shelter lamps, shrine lanterns, command standards, containment wards, harbor arches, and the egg-gate on the skyline.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Dragonkin civilization built around protection, storm memory, rank, refuge, sacred passage, containment, and harbor defense.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Stormfire eggs, dragon scales, shelter beacons, roadside shrines, standards, mist veils, harbor arches, and basilica crossings symbolize dangerous power obligated to protect.

canonLocks: Vyrath’Kai is a mature dragonkin coastal fortress city, not a generic dragon ruin.
The Wonder is Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath.
The Beacon is Shelter Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion.
The five primary districts are Shelter Hall, Shrine / Sacred Place, Military Command, Prison / Containment, and Harbor Campus.

Exact uploaded anchors include S1 Shelter Hall, Shelter Hall Beacon, S1 Shelter Pillar, S1 Shelter Kiosk, Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook, Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, Standard Tower Nook, Prison / Containment Civic Hall, Containment Keep, Mist Veils Monument, Judgment Yard Nook, S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, and S5 Harbor Bridgelet.
provisionalCanon: Vyrath’Kai's strongest civic read is protective stormfire under command.
rejectedReads: harbor without command or containment systems
cautionFlags: Preserve exact shelter and harbor S1/S5 anchor names.
Keep refuge, shrine, command, containment, and harbor systems visible together.

environment.environmentalLogic: The coast is exposed and dangerous; shelters, shrines, command posts, and harbor crossings make survival civic.

skyline: harbor basilica and containment keep along the coast

civilization.people: Dragonkin citizens appear as armored, maritime, and rank-aware civic actors moving through shelter, command, shrine, containment, and harbor spaces.
civilization.governance: Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion governs shelter response, command orders, containment rulings, harbor crossings, and public defense.
civilization.religion: Stormfire eggs, roadside shrines, harbor basilica rites, and protective shelter vows make danger sacred-adjacent.

streets.appearance: Storm-dark coastal streets connect shelter halls, shrine plazas, command bastions, containment keeps, and harbor campus routes under dragon-scale towers and stormfire light.
streets.movement: Movement follows shelter routes, shrine roads, command yards, containment approaches, harbor arches, and bridgelets.
streets.lighting: Stormfire, civic blue, shrine lantern, and harbor light define the city.
streets.weatherEffects: Storms and sea spray make shelters, command response, and harbor crossings essential.
streets.dayCycle: Shelter checks, shrine traffic, command duties, containment review, and harbor movement dominate daytime.
streets.nightCycle: Beacon light, shrine lamps, warded containment, and harbor watch dominate night.

housing.wealthy: Command and harbor residences with dragon-scale relief, sea views, and guarded thresholds.
housing.middle: Shelter, shrine, dock, and command-family homes along civic routes.
housing.poor: Exposed lower coastal housing near work yards, public shelters, and dock routes.

districts.name: Shelter Hall
districts.civicPurpose: Refuge, public warning, and emergency assembly.
districts.visualIdentity: S1 Shelter Hall, Shelter Hall Beacon, S1 Shelter Pillar, S1 Shelter Kiosk.

districts.name: Shrine / Sacred Place
districts.civicPurpose: Public offerings, route blessings, and stormfire vows.
districts.visualIdentity: Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook.

districts.name: Military Command
districts.civicPurpose: Orders, musters, defense, and disciplined dragonkin authority.
districts.visualIdentity: Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, Standard Tower Nook.

districts.name: Prison / Containment
districts.civicPurpose: Warded custody, judgment, and danger management.
districts.visualIdentity: Prison / Containment Civic Hall, Containment Keep, Mist Veils Monument, Judgment Yard Nook.

districts.name: Harbor Campus
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor ritual, public crossing, and maritime identity.
districts.visualIdentity: S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet.

wonder.name: Dragonkin Stormfire Egg-Gate of Vyrath
wonder.symbolicRole: Birth, danger, stormfire, and protected passage made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: Skyline egg-gate burning with stormfire above harbor terraces.
wonder.civicRole: Citywide rites, storm memory, and protective identity.
storyHooks: An egg-gate flame warns before the command bastion does.

beacon.name: Shelter Hall Beacon
beacon.visualRole: Blue signal inside the S1 Shelter Hall complex.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates shelter, command, shrine, containment, and harbor response.
storyHooks: A Beacon state tests who the city shelters first.

seatOfPower.name: Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Dragonkin hierarchy and public protection made government.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central command bastion with scale-pattern stone and standards.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Government for defense, shelter, containment, and harbor order.
storyHooks: A scale-pattern order protects an enemy because the city promised shelter.

majorLandmarks.name: Roadside Shrine
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Public offerings, route blessings, and stormfire vows.
storyHooks: A votive token shows who sought protection before the alarm.

majorLandmarks.name: Command Bastion
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, Standard Tower Nook.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Orders, musters, defense, and disciplined dragonkin authority.
storyHooks: A standard rises before the shelter is full.

majorLandmarks.name: Containment Keep
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Prison / Containment Civic Hall, Containment Keep, Mist Veils Monument, Judgment Yard Nook.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Warded custody, judgment, and danger management.
storyHooks: A judgment yard record proves containment began as refuge.

majorLandmarks.name: Harbor Basilica
majorLandmarks.visualRole: S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Harbor ritual, public crossing, and maritime identity.
storyHooks: A bridgelet crossing becomes a shelter oath.

S2: Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook.

mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep Stormfire Egg-Gate, Shelter Hall Beacon, and Dragon Scale Patterns Command Bastion legible together.
